changes_made: Corrected 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_restricted' to 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_scope_limited' for clarity and specificity
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they map to RBAC configurations, which are crucial for access enforcement. The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern, but 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_restricted' was vague. It was improved to 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_scope_limited' to better describe the intent of restricting role bindings to appropriate scopes.
title: Access Enforcement - P ermits the subject to pass the information to any subject at the same impact o
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(4) focuses on access enforcement, ensuring that information is only passed to subjects with the same impact level. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that RBAC is configured to prevent excessive privileges and restrict access to resources. The functions focus on ensuring that no cluster-admin bindings are present, enforcing least privilege, and restricting role bindings to appropriate subjects.
